step_breakdown.description: Verify source article availability, check format, and collect missing metadata
step_breakdown.risk_notes: Source file location currently MISSING - this is a blocking issue

step_breakdown.description: Parse article content, identify terminology, extract metadata (title, author, date, key topics)
step_breakdown.risk_notes: Article complexity and domain unknown; may contain specialized terminology

step_breakdown.description: Identify article domain (politics, business, technology, etc.), collect domain-specific terminology requirements
step_breakdown.risk_notes: Unknown if user has specific term preferences; may need clarification

step_breakdown.description: Perform first-pass translation from English to Chinese, maintaining article structure
step_breakdown.risk_notes: Quality depends on article complexity and terminology density

step_breakdown.description: Check translation accuracy, terminology consistency, tone appropriateness, and semantic correctness
step_breakdown.risk_notes: Opus provides highest quality review; essential for translation accuracy

step_breakdown.description: Apply proper Markdown syntax, format headings, paragraphs, lists, links, and metadata
Final pass for readability, consistency, formatting correctness, and overall coherence
step_breakdown.risk_notes: Quick final check; mostly automated validation

step_breakdown.description: Generate final Markdown file, validate encoding, check file integrity

haiku.rationale: Fast, cost-efficient for straightforward tasks with clear inputs/outputs

sonnet.rationale: Balanced performance and cost; good for nuanced translation and review work

opus.rationale: Highest quality for critical translation review; ensures accuracy meets standard tier

time_estimation.notes: Assumes article length 500-2000 words; may extend if article is longer or contains specialized technical terminology

critical_risks.description: Source file path is empty; workflow cannot proceed without valid input
critical_risks.mitigation: User must provide source file path before workflow execution

high_risks.description: Article domain, length, and terminology complexity are unknown
high_risks.mitigation: Step 2 (Analysis) will provide detailed assessment; can adjust subsequent steps if needed

high_risks.description: User may have specific requirements for translating key terms (brand names, proper nouns, technical terms)
high_risks.mitigation: Pre-flight questions will collect term preference guidelines

medium_risks.description: Source article may have encoding issues or special characters that affect parsing
medium_risks.mitigation: Step 2 includes encoding detection and validation

medium_risks.description: Output Markdown may need different formatting if used in specific platforms (GitHub, Notion, etc.)
medium_risks.mitigation: Clarify target platform in pre-flight questions

pre_flight_questions_for_user.question: Where is the source English news article located? (Please provide full file path or URL)
pre_flight_questions_for_user.impact: BLOCKING - Cannot proceed without source file
pre_flight_questions_for_user.note: Current value is empty; this is critical

pre_flight_questions_for_user.question: What is the primary domain/subject of this article? (e.g., technology, finance, politics, sports, science)
pre_flight_questions_for_user.impact: MEDIUM - Helps identify domain-specific terminology needs
pre_flight_questions_for_user.note: Impacts translation accuracy for specialized terms

pre_flight_questions_for_user.question: Are there specific terminology preferences or glossaries we should follow? (e.g., company names, technical terms, brand names)
pre_flight_questions_for_user.impact: MEDIUM - Ensures consistency with user's terminology standards
pre_flight_questions_for_user.note: Optional but recommended for professional translations

pre_flight_questions_for_user.question: Where will the translated Markdown file be used? (This affects formatting style)
pre_flight_questions_for_user.impact: LOW - Affects Markdown formatting and metadata style
pre_flight_questions_for_user.note: Optional; standard Markdown will work for most platforms

pre_flight_questions_for_user.question: Should the output include metadata (e.g., title, author, date, source URL)? In what format?
pre_flight_questions_for_user.impact: LOW - Improves document metadata but not essential
pre_flight_questions_for_user.note: Can include YAML frontmatter or inline comments

pre_flight_questions_for_user.question: Since quality tier is 'standard with speed priority', do you have any specific quality concerns? (e.g., accuracy in numbers/dates, style tone, technical accuracy)
pre_flight_questions_for_user.impact: MEDIUM - Guides translation approach and review depth
pre_flight_questions_for_user.note: Speed priority means we optimize for efficiency while maintaining acceptable quality

parallel_capable_steps.step: Step 2 (Analysis) and Step 3 (Domain Assessment) can overlap
Multiple short articles could be batched if processing more than one

sequential_requirements: Step 1 must complete before any other steps (source validation)
Step 4 (Translation) should wait for Step 3 (Guidelines) to complete
Step 7 (Final Review) should be after Step 6
recommended_worker_specialization: Worker 2: Quality Reviewer + Format Specialist (handles steps 5-6, 8)

external_dependencies.suggested_resolution: Obtain full file path or URL for source English news article
Optional but recommended; can use standard terminology if not provided

success_criteria: Source file successfully loaded and validated
Translation completeness: 100% of original content translated
Terminology consistency: All key terms translated consistently
Markdown validity: Output is valid Markdown with no syntax errors
Encoding: UTF-8 or specified encoding correctly applied
Readability: Chinese text is natural and fluent (native speaker standard)

checkpoints.validation: Source file exists and is readable
Output file is valid Markdown and properly encoded

summary_and_recommendations.overall_assessment: Standard translation task with moderate complexity. Primary blocker is missing source file path. Once source is provided and pre-flight questions answered, workflow is straightforward and can be executed within 25-35 minutes using optimized speed-priority approach.
key_recommendations: PRIORITY 1: Resolve missing source file before workflow execution
PRIORITY 2: Collect user's terminology preferences and Markdown platform target
PRIORITY 3: Use sonnet model for translation and haiku for support tasks to optimize cost/speed balance
PRIORITY 4: Implement parallel execution of analysis/review phases for efficiency
summary_and_recommendations.readiness_notes: Once source file path and terminology preferences are provided, workflow can be marked READY_FOR_EXECUTION
